title: leontodon hispidus subsp. montanus
intro: Leontodon hispidus subsp. montanus, also known as the mountain hawk's beard, is a subspecies of the Leontodon hispidus plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Leontodon hispidus subsp. montanus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers to create teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
